2605.05206.title: Taming Outlier Tokens in Diffusion Transformers
2605.05206.paper_summary: We study outlier tokens in Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) for image generation. Prior work has shown that Vision Transformers (ViTs) can produce a small number of high-norm tokens that attract disproportionate attention while carrying limited local information, but their role in generative models remains underexplored. We show that this phenomenon appears in both the encoder and denoiser of modern Representation Autoencoder (RAE)-DiT pipelines: pretrained ViT encoders can produce outlier representations, and DiTs themselves can develop internal outlier tokens, especially in intermediate layers. Moreover, simply masking high-norm tokens does not improve performance, indicating that the problem is not only caused by a few extreme values, but is more closely related to corrupted local patch semantics. To address this issue, we introduce Dual-Stage Registers (DSR), a register-based intervention for both components: trained registers when available, recursive test-time registers otherwise, and diffusion registers for the denoiser. Across ImageNet and large-scale text-to-image generation, these interventions consistently reduce outlier artifacts and improve generation quality. Our results highlight outlier-token control as an important ingredient in building stronger DiTs.
2605.05206.paper_summary_zh: 我們研究擴散Transformer（Diffusion Transformers, DiTs）中的異常標記以進行圖像生成。先前的研究顯示，視覺Transformer（Vision Transformers, ViTs）可以產生少量高範數標記，這些標記吸引了不成比例的注意力，同時攜帶有限的局部信息，但它們在生成模型中的角色仍然未被充分探討。我們展示了這一現象出現在現代表示自編碼器（Representation Autoencoder, RAE）-DiT 流程的編碼器和去噪器中：預訓練的 ViT 編碼器可以產生異常表示，而 DiTs 本身也可以在中間層發展出內部異常標記。此外，僅僅屏蔽高範數標記並不會改善性能，這表明問題不僅是由少數極端值引起的，而是與損壞的局部補丁語義更密切相關。為了解決這個問題，我們引入了雙階段寄存器（Dual-Stage Registers, DSR），這是一種針對兩個組件的基於寄存器的干預：當可用時使用訓練過的寄存器，否則使用遞歸測試時寄存器，並為去噪器使用擴散寄存器。在 ImageNet 和大規模文本到圖像生成中，這些干預措施一致地減少了異常工件並改善了生成質量。我們的結果突顯了異常標記控制作為構建更強 DiTs 的重要成分。
2605.05206.author: Xiaoyu Wu et.al.
2605.05206.authors: Xiaoyu Wu, Yifei Wang, Tsu-Jui Fu, Liang-Chieh Chen, Zhe Gan, Chen Wei
